out_of_scope_note: Pure-ML LLM-alignment interpretability paper analyzing reward models' token-level scoring across ten open-source LLMs. The abstract notes that prompt-framing sensitivity 'mirrors human cognitive biases,' but the analogy is rhetorical only — there is no biological neural recording, no perceptual/decision-making task, and no computational model of the brain. Subject matter (reward modeling, RLHF, vocabulary-space analysis of LLMs) falls under the 'pure-ML papers without biological neural content' OOS category.
